parameter.name: ICE_IGN_DELAY
parameter.short_desc: Cold-start delay after ignition before engaging starter
parameter.long_desc: In case that the ignition takes a moment to be up and running, this parameter can be set to account for that.

parameter.name: ICE_MIN_RUN_RPM
parameter.short_desc: Minimum RPM for engine to be declared running

parameter.name: ICE_RUN_FAULT_D
parameter.short_desc: Fault detection if it stops in running state
parameter.long_desc: Enables restart if a fault is detected during the running state. Otherwise commands continues in running state until given an user request off.

parameter.name: ICE_STRT_ATTEMPT
parameter.short_desc: Number attempts for starting the engine
parameter.long_desc: Number of accepted attempts for starting the engine before declaring a fault.

parameter.name: ICE_STRT_DUR
parameter.short_desc: Duration of single starting attempt (excl. choking)
parameter.long_desc: Maximum expected time for startup before declaring timeout.

parameter.name: ICE_STRT_THR
parameter.long_desc: During the choking and the starting phase, the throttle value is set to this value.

parameter.name: ICE_THR_SLEW
parameter.long_desc: Maximum rate of change of throttle value per second.

parameter.name: MAV_0_FLOW_CTRL
parameter.short_desc: Enable serial flow control for instance 0
parameter.long_desc: This is used to force flow control on or off for the the mavlink instance. By default it is auto detected. Use when auto detection fails.

parameter.name: MAV_0_FORWARD
parameter.short_desc: Enable MAVLink Message forwarding for instance 0
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, forward incoming MAVLink messages to other MAVLink ports if the message is either broadcast or the target is not the autopilot. This allows for example a GCS to talk to a camera that is connected to the autopilot via MAVLink (on a different link than the GCS).

parameter.name: MAV_0_HL_FREQ
parameter.short_desc: Configures the frequency of HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 0
parameter.long_desc: Positive real value that configures the transmission frequency of the HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 0, configured in iridium mode. This parameter has no effect if the instance mode is different from iridium.

parameter.name: MAV_0_MODE
parameter.long_desc: The MAVLink Mode defines the set of streamed messages (for example the vehicle's attitude) and their sending rates.

parameter.name: MAV_0_RADIO_CTL
parameter.short_desc: Enable software throttling of mavlink on instance 0
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, MAVLink messages will be throttled according to `txbuf` field reported by radio_status. Requires a radio to send the mavlink message RADIO_STATUS.

parameter.name: MAV_0_RATE
parameter.short_desc: Maximum MAVLink sending rate for instance 0
parameter.long_desc: Configure the maximum sending rate for the MAVLink streams in Bytes/sec. If the configured streams exceed the maximum rate, the sending rate of each stream is automatically decreased. If this is set to 0 a value of half of the theoretical maximum bandwidth is used. This corresponds to baudrate/20 Bytes/s (baudrate/10 = maximum data rate on 8N1-configured links).

parameter.name: MAV_1_FLOW_CTRL
parameter.short_desc: Enable serial flow control for instance 1
parameter.long_desc: This is used to force flow control on or off for the the mavlink instance. By default it is auto detected. Use when auto detection fails.

parameter.name: MAV_1_FORWARD
parameter.short_desc: Enable MAVLink Message forwarding for instance 1
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, forward incoming MAVLink messages to other MAVLink ports if the message is either broadcast or the target is not the autopilot. This allows for example a GCS to talk to a camera that is connected to the autopilot via MAVLink (on a different link than the GCS).

parameter.name: MAV_1_HL_FREQ
parameter.short_desc: Configures the frequency of HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 1
parameter.long_desc: Positive real value that configures the transmission frequency of the HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 1, configured in iridium mode. This parameter has no effect if the instance mode is different from iridium.

parameter.name: MAV_1_MODE
parameter.long_desc: The MAVLink Mode defines the set of streamed messages (for example the vehicle's attitude) and their sending rates.

parameter.name: MAV_1_RADIO_CTL
parameter.short_desc: Enable software throttling of mavlink on instance 1
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, MAVLink messages will be throttled according to `txbuf` field reported by radio_status. Requires a radio to send the mavlink message RADIO_STATUS.

parameter.name: MAV_1_RATE
parameter.short_desc: Maximum MAVLink sending rate for instance 1
parameter.long_desc: Configure the maximum sending rate for the MAVLink streams in Bytes/sec. If the configured streams exceed the maximum rate, the sending rate of each stream is automatically decreased. If this is set to 0 a value of half of the theoretical maximum bandwidth is used. This corresponds to baudrate/20 Bytes/s (baudrate/10 = maximum data rate on 8N1-configured links).

parameter.name: MAV_2_FLOW_CTRL
parameter.short_desc: Enable serial flow control for instance 2
parameter.long_desc: This is used to force flow control on or off for the the mavlink instance. By default it is auto detected. Use when auto detection fails.

parameter.name: MAV_2_FORWARD
parameter.short_desc: Enable MAVLink Message forwarding for instance 2
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, forward incoming MAVLink messages to other MAVLink ports if the message is either broadcast or the target is not the autopilot. This allows for example a GCS to talk to a camera that is connected to the autopilot via MAVLink (on a different link than the GCS).

parameter.name: MAV_2_HL_FREQ
parameter.short_desc: Configures the frequency of HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 2
parameter.long_desc: Positive real value that configures the transmission frequency of the HIGH_LATENCY2 stream for instance 2, configured in iridium mode. This parameter has no effect if the instance mode is different from iridium.

parameter.name: MAV_2_MODE
parameter.long_desc: The MAVLink Mode defines the set of streamed messages (for example the vehicle's attitude) and their sending rates.

parameter.name: MAV_2_RADIO_CTL
parameter.short_desc: Enable software throttling of mavlink on instance 2
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, MAVLink messages will be throttled according to `txbuf` field reported by radio_status. Requires a radio to send the mavlink message RADIO_STATUS.

parameter.name: MAV_2_RATE
parameter.short_desc: Maximum MAVLink sending rate for instance 2
parameter.long_desc: Configure the maximum sending rate for the MAVLink streams in Bytes/sec. If the configured streams exceed the maximum rate, the sending rate of each stream is automatically decreased. If this is set to 0 a value of half of the theoretical maximum bandwidth is used. This corresponds to baudrate/20 Bytes/s (baudrate/10 = maximum data rate on 8N1-configured links).

parameter.name: MAV_S_FORWARD
parameter.long_desc: TELEM2 on Skynode only.

parameter.name: MAV_S_MODE
parameter.short_desc: MAVLink Mode for SOM to FMU communication channel
parameter.long_desc: The MAVLink Mode defines the set of streamed messages (for example the vehicle's attitude) and their sending rates.

parameter.name: BMM350_AVG
parameter.long_desc: Defines which averaging mode to use during data polling.

parameter.name: BMM350_DRIVE
parameter.long_desc: This setting helps avoid signal problems like overshoot or undershoot.

parameter.name: BMM350_ODR
parameter.long_desc: Defines which ODR rate to use during data polling.

parameter.name: MODALAI_CONFIG
parameter.long_desc: This can be set to indicate that drone behavior needs to be changed to match a custom setting

parameter.name: MC_RAPTOR_ENABLE
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, the Raptor flight mode will be available. Please set MC_RAPTOR_OFFB according to your use case.

parameter.name: MC_RAPTOR_INTREF
parameter.short_desc: Use internal reference instead of trajectory_setpoint
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, instead of using the trajectory_setpoint, the position and yaw of the vehicle at the point when the Raptor mode is activated will be used as reference. Use `mc_raptor intref lissajous <A> <B> <C> <fa> <fb> <fc> <duration> <ramp>` to configure the trajectory.

parameter.name: MC_RAPTOR_OFFB
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, the Raptor mode will replace the Offboard mode. If disabled, the Raptor mode will be available as a separate external mode. In the latter case, Raptor will just hold the position, without requiring external setpoints. When Raptor replaces the Offboard mode, it requires external setpoints to be activated.

parameter.name: MC_RAPTOR_VERBOS
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, the Raptor flight mode will print verbose output to the console.

parameter.name: OSD_CH_HEIGHT
parameter.long_desc: Controls the vertical position of the crosshair display. Resolution is limited by OSD to 15 discrete values. Negative values will display the crosshairs below the horizon

parameter.name: OSD_DWELL_TIME
parameter.long_desc: Amount of time in milliseconds to dwell at the beginning of the display, when scrolling.

parameter.name: OSD_LOG_LEVEL
parameter.long_desc: Minimum security of log level to display on the OSD.

parameter.name: OSD_RC_STICK
parameter.long_desc: Forward RC stick input to VTX when disarmed

parameter.name: OSD_SCROLL_RATE
parameter.long_desc: Scroll rate in milliseconds for OSD messages longer than available character width. This is lower-bounded by the nominal loop rate of this module.

parameter.name: OSD_SYMBOLS
parameter.long_desc: Configure / toggle support display options.

parameter.name: PD_GRIPPER_TO
parameter.short_desc: Timeout for successful gripper actuation acknowledgement
parameter.long_desc: Maximum time Gripper will wait while the successful griper actuation isn't recognised. If the gripper has no feedback sensor, it will simply wait for this time before considering gripper actuation successful and publish a 'VehicleCommandAck' signaling successful gripper action

parameter.name: RC_CRSF_TEL_EN
parameter.long_desc: Crossfire telemetry enable

parameter.name: RC_GHST_TEL_EN
parameter.long_desc: Ghost telemetry enable

parameter.name: RC_INPUT_PROTO
parameter.long_desc: Select your RC input protocol or auto to scan.

parameter.name: RBCLW_ADDRESS
parameter.short_desc: Address of the ESC on the bus
parameter.long_desc: The ESC has to be configured to have an address from 0x80 to 0x87. This parameter needs to match the configured value.

parameter.name: RBCLW_COUNTS_REV
parameter.short_desc: Number of encoder counts for one wheel revolution
parameter.long_desc: The default value of 1200 corresponds to the default configuration of the Aion R1 rover.

parameter.name: RA_ACC_RAD_GAIN
parameter.long_desc: The geometric ideal acceptance radius is multiplied by this factor to account for kinematic and dynamic effects. Higher value -> The rover starts to cut the corner earlier.

parameter.name: RA_ACC_RAD_MAX
parameter.short_desc: Maximum acceptance radius for the waypoints
parameter.long_desc: The controller scales the acceptance radius based on the angle between the previous, current and next waypoint. Higher value -> smoother trajectory at the cost of how close the rover gets to the waypoint (Set to -1 to disable corner cutting).

parameter.name: RA_STR_RATE_LIM
parameter.long_desc: Set to -1 to disable.

parameter.name: RA_WHEEL_BASE
parameter.long_desc: Distance from the front to the rear axle.

parameter.name: RD_TRANS_DRV_TRN
parameter.short_desc: Yaw error threshhold to switch from driving to spot turning
parameter.long_desc: This threshold is used for the state machine to switch from driving to turning based on the error between the desired and actual yaw. It is also used as the threshold whether the rover should come to a smooth stop at the next waypoint. This slow down effect is active if the angle between the line segments from prevWP-currWP and currWP-nextWP is smaller then 180 - RD_TRANS_DRV_TRN.

parameter.name: RD_TRANS_TRN_DRV
parameter.short_desc: Yaw error threshhold to switch from spot turning to driving
parameter.long_desc: This threshold is used for the state machine to switch from turning to driving based on the error between the desired and actual yaw.

parameter.name: RD_WHEEL_TRACK
parameter.long_desc: Distance from the center of the right wheel to the center of the left wheel.

parameter.name: RD_YAW_STK_GAIN
parameter.short_desc: Yaw stick gain for Manual mode
parameter.long_desc: Assign value <1.0 to decrease stick response for yaw control.

parameter.name: RM_COURSE_CTL_TH
parameter.short_desc: Threshold to update course control in manual position mode
parameter.long_desc: Threshold for the angle between the active cruise direction and the cruise direction given by the stick inputs. This can be understood as a deadzone for the combined stick inputs for forward/backwards and lateral speed which defines a course direction.

parameter.name: RM_WHEEL_TRACK
parameter.long_desc: Distance from the center of the right wheel to the center of the left wheel.

parameter.name: RM_YAW_STK_GAIN
parameter.short_desc: Yaw stick gain for Manual mode
parameter.long_desc: Assign value <1.0 to decrease stick response for yaw control.

parameter.name: SDLOG_ALGORITHM
parameter.long_desc: Selects the algorithm used for logfile encryption

parameter.name: SDLOG_BACKEND
parameter.long_desc: If no logging is set the logger will not be started. Set bits true to enable: 0: SD card logging 1: Mavlink logging

parameter.name: SDLOG_BOOT_BAT
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, logging will not start from boot if battery power is not detected (e.g. powered via USB on a test bench). This prevents extraneous flight logs from being created during bench testing. Note that this only applies to log-from-boot modes. This has no effect on arm-based modes.

parameter.name: SDLOG_DIRS_MAX
parameter.short_desc: Maximum number of log directories to keep
parameter.long_desc: If there are more log directories than this value, the system will delete the oldest directories during startup. In addition, the system will delete old logs if there is not enough free space left. The minimum amount is 300 MB. If this is set to 0, old directories will only be removed if the free space falls below the minimum. Note: this does not apply to mission log files.

parameter.name: SDLOG_EXCH_KEY
parameter.long_desc: If the logfile is encrypted using a symmetric key algorithm, the used encryption key is generated at logging start and stored on the sdcard RSA2048 encrypted using this key.

parameter.name: SDLOG_KEY
parameter.long_desc: Selects the key in keystore, used for encrypting the log. When using a symmetric encryption algorithm, the key is generated at logging start and kept stored in this index. For symmetric algorithms, the key is volatile and valid only for the duration of logging. The key is stored in encrypted format on the sdcard alongside the logfile, using an RSA2048 key defined by the SDLOG_EXCHANGE_KEY

parameter.name: SDLOG_MISSION
parameter.long_desc: If enabled, a small additional "mission" log file will be written to the SD card. The log contains just those messages that are useful for tasks like generating flight statistics and geotagging. The different modes can be used to further reduce the logged data (and thus the log file size). For example, choose geotagging mode to only log data required for geotagging. Note that the normal/full log is still created, and contains all the data in the mission log (and more).

parameter.name: SDLOG_MODE
parameter.long_desc: Determines when to start and stop logging. By default, logging is started when arming the system, and stopped when disarming. Note: The logging start/end points that can be configured here only apply to SD logging. The mavlink backend is started/stopped independently of these points.

parameter.name: SDLOG_PROFILE
parameter.long_desc: This integer bitmask controls the set and rates of logged topics. The default allows for general log analysis while keeping the log file size reasonably small. Enabling multiple sets leads to higher bandwidth requirements and larger log files. Set bits true to enable: 0 : Default set (used for general log analysis) 1 : Full rate estimator (EKF2) replay topics 2 : Topics for thermal calibration (high rate raw IMU and Baro sensor data) 3 : Topics for system identification (high rate actuator control and IMU data) 4 : Full rates for analysis of fast maneuvers (RC, attitude, rates and actuators) 5 : Debugging topics (debug_*.msg topics, for custom code) 6 : Topics for sensor comparison (low rate raw IMU, Baro and magnetometer data) 7 : Topics for computer vision and collision prevention 8 : Raw FIFO high-rate IMU (Gyro) 9 : Raw FIFO high-rate IMU (Accel) 10: Logging of mavlink tunnel message (useful for payload communication debugging)

parameter.name: SDLOG_UTC_OFFSET
parameter.long_desc: the difference in hours and minutes from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) for a your place and date. for example, In case of South Korea(UTC+09:00), UTC offset is 540 min (9*60) refer to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_UTC_time_offsets

parameter.name: SDLOG_UUID
parameter.long_desc: If set to 1, add an ID to the log, which uniquely identifies the vehicle

parameter.name: CAL_ACC0_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the accelerometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_ACC0_ROT
parameter.long_desc: An internal sensor will force a value of -1, so a GCS should only attempt to configure the rotation if the value is greater than or equal to zero.

parameter.name: CAL_ACC1_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the accelerometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_ACC1_ROT
parameter.long_desc: An internal sensor will force a value of -1, so a GCS should only attempt to configure the rotation if the value is greater than or equal to zero.

parameter.name: CAL_BARO0_ID

parameter.name: CAL_BARO1_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the barometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_BARO2_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the barometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_BARO3_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the barometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_GYRO0_ID

parameter.name: CAL_GYRO0_ROT
parameter.long_desc: An internal sensor will force a value of -1, so a GCS should only attempt to configure the rotation if the value is greater than or equal to zero.

parameter.name: CAL_GYRO1_ID

parameter.name: CAL_GYRO1_ROT

parameter.name: CAL_GYRO3_ROT
parameter.long_desc: An internal sensor will force a value of -1, so a GCS should only attempt to configure the rotation if the value is greater than or equal to zero.

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the magnetometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_PITCH
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG0_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_ROLL
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG0_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_ROT
parameter.long_desc: An internal sensor will force a value of -1, so a GCS should only attempt to configure the rotation if the value is greater than or equal to zero. Set to "Custom Euler Angle" to define the rotation using CAL_MAG0_ROLL, CAL_MAG0_PITCH and CAL_MAG0_YAW.

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_XCOMP
parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between X component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_XODIAG
parameter.short_desc: Magnetometer 0 X-axis off diagonal scale factor

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_YAW
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG0_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_YCOMP
parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between Y component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_YODIAG
parameter.short_desc: Magnetometer 0 Y-axis off diagonal scale factor

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_ZCOMP
parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between Z component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.name: CAL_MAG0_ZODIAG
parameter.short_desc: Magnetometer 0 Z-axis off diagonal scale factor

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the magnetometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_PITCH
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG1_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_ROLL
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG1_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_ROT

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_XCOMP
parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between X component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_YCOMP
parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between Y component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.long_desc: Coefficient describing linear relationship between Z component of magnetometer in body frame axis and either current or throttle depending on value of CAL_MAG_COMP_TYP. Unit for throttle-based compensation is [G] and for current-based compensation [G/kA]

parameter.name: CAL_MAG1_ZODIAG

parameter.name: CAL_MAG2_ID
parameter.long_desc: Device ID of the magnetometer this calibration applies to.

parameter.name: CAL_MAG2_PITCH
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG2_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: CAL_MAG2_ROLL
parameter.long_desc: Setting this parameter changes CAL_MAG2_ROT to "Custom Euler Angle"

parameter.name: GRF_RATE_CFG
parameter.long_desc: The Lightware GRF distance sensor can increase the update rate to enable greater resolution.

parameter.name: GRF_SENS_MODEL
parameter.long_desc: GRF Sensor Model used to distinush between the GRF250 and GRF500 since both have different max distance range.

parameter.name: HEATER1_IMU_ID
parameter.short_desc: The ID of the IMU controlled by heater 1
parameter.long_desc: Specifies the sensor device ID (DEVID) that this heater instance controls. -1 disables this heater instance. If set to 0, auto-select is only supported when HEATER_NUM == 1. On boards with multiple heater outputs, a valid DEVID must be configured for each heater to ensure a 1:1 mapping between heater output and IMU.

parameter.name: HEATER1_TEMP
parameter.long_desc: Specify the target stable temperature (in degrees Celsius) for the IMU. It is generally recommended to set this between 40°C and 60°C, which must be higher than the maximum ambient temperature.

parameter.name: HEATER1_TEMP_FF
parameter.long_desc: Used to predict the baseline power consumption required to maintain temperature, helping to reduce adjustment time.

parameter.name: HEATER1_TEMP_I
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 1 integrator gain value
parameter.long_desc: Integral gain is used to eliminate steady-state error, ensuring that the temperature ultimately reaches the setpoint target.

parameter.name: HEATER1_TEMP_P
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 1 proportional gain value
parameter.long_desc: The proportional gain determines how quickly the controller responds to temperature deviations.

parameter.name: HEATER2_IMU_ID
parameter.short_desc: The ID of the IMU controlled by heater 2
parameter.long_desc: Specifies the sensor device ID (DEVID) that this heater instance controls. -1 disables this heater instance. If set to 0, auto-select is only supported when HEATER_NUM == 1. On boards with multiple heater outputs, a valid DEVID must be configured for each heater to ensure a 1:1 mapping between heater output and IMU.

parameter.name: HEATER2_TEMP
parameter.long_desc: Specify the target stable temperature (in degrees Celsius) for the IMU. It is generally recommended to set this between 40°C and 60°C, which must be higher than the maximum ambient temperature.

parameter.name: HEATER2_TEMP_FF
parameter.long_desc: Used to predict the baseline power consumption required to maintain temperature, helping to reduce adjustment time.

parameter.name: HEATER2_TEMP_I
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 2 integrator gain value
parameter.long_desc: Integral gain is used to eliminate steady-state error, ensuring that the temperature ultimately reaches the setpoint target.

parameter.name: HEATER2_TEMP_P
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 2 proportional gain value
parameter.long_desc: The proportional gain determines how quickly the controller responds to temperature deviations.

parameter.name: HEATER3_IMU_ID
parameter.short_desc: The ID of the IMU controlled by heater 3
parameter.long_desc: Specifies the sensor device ID (DEVID) that this heater instance controls. -1 disables this heater instance. If set to 0, auto-select is only supported when HEATER_NUM == 1. On boards with multiple heater outputs, a valid DEVID must be configured for each heater to ensure a 1:1 mapping between heater output and IMU.

parameter.name: HEATER3_TEMP
parameter.long_desc: Specify the target stable temperature (in degrees Celsius) for the IMU. It is generally recommended to set this between 40°C and 60°C, which must be higher than the maximum ambient temperature.

parameter.name: HEATER3_TEMP_FF
parameter.long_desc: Used to predict the baseline power consumption required to maintain temperature, helping to reduce adjustment time.

parameter.name: HEATER3_TEMP_I
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 3 integrator gain value
parameter.long_desc: Integral gain is used to eliminate steady-state error, ensuring that the temperature ultimately reaches the setpoint target.

parameter.name: HEATER3_TEMP_P
parameter.short_desc: IMU heater controller 3 proportional gain value
parameter.long_desc: The proportional gain determines how quickly the controller responds to temperature deviations.

parameter.name: ILABS_MODE
parameter.long_desc: Configures whether the driver outputs only raw sensor output (the default), or additionally supplies INS data such as position and velocity estimates.

parameter.name: MS_ACCEL_RANGE
parameter.long_desc: -1 = Will not be configured, and will use the device default range. Ranges vary by device and map to integer codes. Check the device's [User Manual](https://www.hbkworld.com/en/products/transducers/inertial-sensors#!ref_microstrain.com) for supported ranges and set the corresponding integer.

parameter.name: MS_ALIGNMENT
parameter.long_desc: Select the source of heading alignment.

bitmask.bit: GNSS kinematic (requires motion, e.g. a GNSS velocity)
External Heading (first valid external heading will be used to initialize the filter)

parameter.name: MS_BARO_RATE_HZ
parameter.long_desc: Barometer data rate (Hz). Valid rates: 0 or any factor of 1000.

parameter.name: MS_EHEAD_YAW
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the z axis. Requires MS_EXT_HEAD_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_EMAG_PTCH
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the y axis. Requires MS_EXT_MAG_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_EMAG_ROLL
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the x axis. Requires MS_EXT_MAG_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_EMAG_UNCERT
parameter.long_desc: The 1-sigma uncertainty (in Gauss) for all axes, which will remain constant across all aiding measurements. Requires MS_EXT_MAG_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_EMAG_YAW
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the z axis. Requires MS_EXT_MAG_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_EXT_HEAD_EN
parameter.long_desc: Toggles external heading as an aiding measurement. If enabled, the filter will be configured to accept external heading as an aiding meaurement.

parameter.name: MS_EXT_MAG_EN
parameter.long_desc: Toggles external magnetometer aiding in the device filter.

parameter.name: MS_FILT_RATE_HZ
parameter.long_desc: The rate at which the INS data is published (Hz). Valid rates: 0 or any factor of 1000.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_AID_SRC
parameter.long_desc: Select the source of gnss aiding (GNSS/INS).

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF1_X
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the X direction for the external GNSS receiver. In the case of a dual antenna setup, this is antenna 1.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF1_Y
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the Y direction for the external GNSS receiver. In the case of a dual antenna setup, this is antenna 1.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF1_Z
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the Z direction for the external GNSS receiver. In the case of a dual antenna setup, this is antenna 1.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF2_X
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the X direction for antenna 2 This will only be used if the device supports a dual antenna setup.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF2_Y
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the Y direction for antenna 2. This will only be used if the device supports a dual antenna setup.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_OFF2_Z
parameter.long_desc: Lever arm offset (m) in the X direction for antenna 2. This will only be used if the device supports a dual antenna setup.

parameter.name: MS_GNSS_RATE_HZ
parameter.long_desc: GNSS receiver 1 and 2 data rate (Hz). Valid rates: 0, 1 or 5.

parameter.name: MS_GYRO_RANGE
parameter.long_desc: -1 = Will not be configured, and will use the device default range. Ranges vary by device and map to integer codes. Check the device's [User Manual](https://www.hbkworld.com/en/products/transducers/inertial-sensors#!ref_microstrain.com) for supported ranges and set the corresponding integer.

parameter.name: MS_IMU_RATE_HZ
parameter.long_desc: Accelerometer and Gyroscope data rate (Hz). Valid rates: 0 or any factor of 1000.

parameter.name: MS_INT_HEAD_EN
parameter.long_desc: Toggles internal heading as an aiding measurement. If dual antennas are supported (CV7-GNSS/INS). The filter will be configured to use dual antenna heading as an aiding measurement.

parameter.name: MS_INT_MAG_EN
parameter.long_desc: Toggles internal magnetometer aiding in the device filter.

parameter.name: MS_MAG_RATE_HZ
parameter.long_desc: Magnetometer data rate (Hz). Valid rates: 0 or any factor of 1000.

parameter.name: MS_MODE
parameter.long_desc: Sensor mode publishes raw IMU data to be used by EKF2. INS data from the device is published to the external INS topics. INS mode publishes the INS data to the vehicle topics to be used for navigation.

parameter.name: MS_OFLW_OFF_X
parameter.long_desc: Offset (m) in the X direction if an Optical Flow sensor is connected. Requires MS_OPT_FLOW_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_OFLW_OFF_Y
parameter.long_desc: Offset (m) in the Y direction if an Optical Flow sensor is connected. Requires MS_OPT_FLOW_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_OFLW_OFF_Z
parameter.long_desc: Offset (m) in the Z direction if an Optical Flow sensor is connected. Requires MS_OPT_FLOW_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_OFLW_UNCERT
parameter.long_desc: The 1-sigma uncertainty (in m/s) for the X and Y axes, which will remain constant across all aiding measurements. The Z axis is not used for aiding. Requires MS_OPT_FLOW_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_OPT_FLOW_EN
parameter.long_desc: Toggles body frame velocity as an aiding measurement. The driver uses the body frame velocity from the optical flow sensor as the aiding measurements.

parameter.name: MS_SENSOR_PTCH
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the y axis. Requires MS_SVT_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_SENSOR_ROLL
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the x axis. Requires MS_SVT_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_SENSOR_YAW
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the device (Radians) with respect to the vehicle frame around the z axis. Requires MS_SVT_EN to be enabled to be used.

parameter.name: MS_SVT_EN
parameter.short_desc: Enables Microstrain sensor to vehicle transform
parameter.long_desc: If the sensor has a different orientation with respect to the vehicle. This will enable a transform to correct itself. The transform is described by MS_SENSOR_ROLL, MS_SENSOR_PITCH, MS_SENSOR_YAW.

parameter.name: SBG_BAUDRATE
parameter.long_desc: Baudrate used by default for serial communication between PX4 and SBG Systems INS through sbgECom driver.

parameter.name: SBG_CONFIGURE_EN
parameter.long_desc: Enable SBG Systems INS configuration through sbgECom driver on start.

parameter.name: SBG_MODE
parameter.long_desc: Modes available for sbgECom driver. In Sensors Only mode, use external IMU and magnetometer. In GNSS mode, use external GNSS in addition to sensors only mode. In INS mode, use external Kalman Filter in addition to GNSS mode. In INS mode, requires EKF2_EN 0. Keeping both enabled can lead to an unexpected behavior and vehicle instability.

parameter.name: SENS_CM8JL65_R_0
parameter.long_desc: Distance Sensor Rotation as MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION enum

parameter.name: SENS_EN_TMP102
parameter.long_desc: Enable the driver for the TMP102 temperature sensor

parameter.name: SENS_TFMINI_HW
parameter.long_desc: Models differ in range and FoV.

parameter.name: SF45_ORIENT_CFG
parameter.long_desc: The SF45 mounted facing upward or downward on the frame

parameter.name: SF45_UPDATE_CFG
parameter.long_desc: The SF45 sets the update rate in Hz to allow greater resolution

parameter.name: SF45_YAW_CFG
parameter.long_desc: The usb port on the sensor indicates 180deg, opposite usb is forward facing

parameter.name: VN_MODE
parameter.long_desc: INS or sensors

parameter.name: SEP_AUTO_CONFIG
parameter.long_desc: By default, the receiver is automatically configured. Sometimes it may be used for multiple purposes. If the offered parameters aren't sufficient, this parameter can be disabled to have full control of the receiver configuration. A good way to use this is to enable automatic configuration, let the receiver be configured, and then disable it to make manual adjustments.

parameter.name: SEP_CONST_USAGE
parameter.long_desc: Choice of which constellations the receiver should use for PVT computation. When this is 0, the constellation usage isn't changed.

parameter.name: SEP_DUMP_COMM
parameter.long_desc: Log raw communication between the driver and connected receivers. For example, "To receiver" will log all commands and corrections sent by the driver to the receiver.

parameter.name: SEP_HARDW_SETUP
parameter.short_desc: Setup and expected use of the hardware
parameter.long_desc: Setup and expected use of the hardware. - Default: Use two receivers as completely separate instances. - Moving base: Use two receivers in a rover & moving base setup for heading.

parameter.name: SEP_LOG_FORCE
parameter.short_desc: Whether to overwrite or add to existing logging
parameter.long_desc: When the receiver is already set up to log data, this decides whether extra logged data should be added or overwrite existing data.

parameter.name: SEP_LOG_HZ
parameter.long_desc: Select the frequency at which the connected receiver should log data to its internal storage.

parameter.name: SEP_LOG_LEVEL
parameter.long_desc: Select the level of detail that needs to be logged by the receiver.

parameter.name: SEP_OUTP_HZ
parameter.short_desc: Output frequency of main SBF blocks
parameter.long_desc: The output frequency of the main SBF blocks needed for PVT information.

parameter.name: SEP_PITCH_OFFS
parameter.short_desc: Pitch offset for dual antenna GPS
parameter.long_desc: Vertical offsets can be compensated for by adjusting the Pitch offset. Note that this can be interpreted as the "roll" angle in case the antennas are aligned along the perpendicular axis. This occurs in situations where the two antenna ARPs may not be exactly at the same height in the vehicle reference frame. Since pitch is defined as the right-handed rotation about the vehicle Y axis, a situation where the main antenna is mounted lower than the aux antenna (assuming the default antenna setup) will result in a positive pitch.

parameter.name: SEP_SAT_INFO
parameter.long_desc: Enable publication of satellite info (ORB_ID(satellite_info)) if possible.

parameter.name: SEP_STREAM_LOG
parameter.short_desc: Logging stream used during automatic configuration
parameter.long_desc: The stream the autopilot sets up on the receiver to output the logging data. Set this to another value if the default stream is already used for another purpose.

parameter.name: SEP_STREAM_MAIN
parameter.short_desc: Main stream used during automatic configuration
parameter.long_desc: The stream the autopilot sets up on the receiver to output the main data. Set this to another value if the default stream is already used for another purpose.

parameter.name: SEP_YAW_OFFS
parameter.short_desc: Heading/Yaw offset for dual antenna GPS
parameter.long_desc: Heading offset angle for dual antenna GPS setups that support heading estimation. Set this to 0 if the antennas are parallel to the forward-facing direction of the vehicle and the rover antenna is in front. The offset angle increases clockwise. Set this to 90 if the rover antenna is placed on the right side of the vehicle and the moving base antenna is on the left side.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_ESC_IFACE
parameter.short_desc: Which CAN interfaces to output ESC messages on
parameter.long_desc: Since ESC messages are high priority and sent at a high rate, it is recommended to only enable the interfaces that are actually used. Otherwise, the ESC messages will arbitrate lower priority messages and starve other nodes on the bus.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_LGT_FN0
parameter.long_desc: Function for light 0. UAVCAN_LGT_MODE determines when the first option or second option is active Off/On.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_LGT_FN1
parameter.long_desc: Function for light 1. UAVCAN_LGT_MODE determines when the first option or second option is active Off/On.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_LGT_ID0
parameter.long_desc: specifies the light_id value for light 0 in UAVCAN LightsCommand messages. This determines which physical LED responds to commands for this light slot.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_LGT_ID1
parameter.long_desc: specifies the light_id value for light 1 in UAVCAN LightsCommand messages. This determines which physical LED responds to commands for this light slot.

parameter.name: UAVCAN_LGT_NUM
parameter.short_desc: Number of UAVCAN lights to configure
parameter.long_desc: Number of lights to control via UAVCAN LightsCommand messages. Set to 0 to disable UAVCAN light control. Each light uses two parameters: LGT_IDx for the light_id and LGT_FNx for the function.

parameter.name: UWB_INIT_OFF_X
parameter.short_desc: UWB sensor X offset in body frame
parameter.long_desc: UWB sensor positioning in relation to Drone in NED. X offset. A Positive offset results in a Position o

parameter.name: UWB_INIT_OFF_Y
parameter.short_desc: UWB sensor Y offset in body frame
parameter.long_desc: UWB sensor positioning in relation to Drone in NED. Y offset.

parameter.name: UWB_INIT_OFF_Z
parameter.short_desc: UWB sensor Z offset in body frame
parameter.long_desc: UWB sensor positioning in relation to Drone in NED. Z offset.

parameter.name: UWB_SENS_ROT
parameter.long_desc: The orientation of the sensor relative to the forward direction of the body frame. Look up table in src/lib/conversion/rotation.h Default position is the antannaes downward facing, UWB board parallel with body frame.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_DOM_ID
parameter.long_desc: uXRCE-DDS domain ID

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_FLCTRL
parameter.short_desc: Enable serial flow control for UXRCE interface
parameter.long_desc: This is used to enable flow control for the serial uXRCE instance. Used for reliable high bandwidth communication.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_KEY
parameter.long_desc: uXRCE-DDS key, must be different from zero. In a single agent - multi client configuration, each client must have a unique session key.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_NS_IDX
parameter.long_desc: Defines an index-based namespace for DDS messages, e.g, uav_0, uav_1, up to uav_9999 A value less than zero leaves the namespace empty

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_PTCFG
parameter.long_desc: Set the participant configuration on the Agent's system. 0: Use the default configuration. 1: Restrict messages to localhost (use in combination with ROS_LOCALHOST_ONLY=1). 2: Use a custom participant with the profile name "px4_participant".

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_RX_TO
parameter.long_desc: Specifies after how many seconds without receiving data the DDS connection is reestablished. A value less than one disables the RX rate timeout.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_SYNCC
parameter.long_desc: When enabled along with UXRCE_DDS_SYNCT, uxrce_dds_client will set the system clock using the agents UTC timestamp.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_SYNCT
parameter.long_desc: When enabled, uxrce_dds_client will synchronize the timestamps of the incoming and outgoing messages measuring the offset between the Agent OS time and the PX4 time.

parameter.name: UXRCE_DDS_TX_TO
parameter.long_desc: Specifies after how many seconds without sending data the DDS connection is reestablished. A value less than one disables the TX rate timeout.

parameter.name: VTX_BAND
parameter.long_desc: VTX table band 1-24

parameter.name: VTX_CHANNEL
parameter.long_desc: VTX table channel 1-16

parameter.name: VTX_DEVICE
parameter.long_desc: Specific VTX device useful for workarounds and optimizations

parameter.name: VTX_FREQUENCY
parameter.long_desc: If the VTX frequency is set, it will overwrite the band and channel settings. Set to 0 to use band and channel settings.

parameter.name: VTX_MAP_CONFIG
parameter.long_desc: Configure how VTX settings are controlled. Options include using MSP commands, AUX channels or a combination of both. To use MSP commands, you must use a CRSF receiver with MSP support enabled in the PX4 build.

values.value: MSP for Power, Band and Channel
MSP for Band and Channel, AUX for Power
MSP for Power, AUX for Band and Channel
AUX for Power, Band and Channel

parameter.name: VTX_PIT_MODE
parameter.long_desc: VTX pit mode reduces power to the minimum

parameter.name: VTX_POWER
parameter.long_desc: VTX transmission power level 1-16

parameter.name: VTQ_ARM_BEHAVE
parameter.short_desc: The triggered behavior on PX4 arm
parameter.long_desc: The behavior triggered when the flight controller arms. You have the option to use your motors' arming behaviors, or to force all of your motors to arm

parameter.name: VTQ_BAUD
parameter.long_desc: The baud rate (in bits per second) used by the serial port connected with IQUART communication

parameter.name: VTQ_CONTROL_MODE
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - The module's control mechanism
parameter.long_desc: PWM Mode: Commands a fraction of battery voltage. This changes as the battery voltage changes. This is the least safe mode because the upper throttle limit is determined by the battery voltage. Voltage Mode: Commands a voltage. The motor will behave the same way throughout the life of a battery, assuming the commanded voltage is less than the battery voltage. You must set the MAX_VOLTS parameter. Velocity Mode: Closed-loop, commands a velocity. The controller will adjust the applied voltage so that the motor spins at the commanded velocity. This mode has faster reaction times. Only use this if you know the properties of your propeller. You must set the MAX_VELOCITY parameter.

parameter.name: VTQ_DISARM_TRIG
parameter.short_desc: The triggered behavior sent to the motors on PX4 disarm
parameter.long_desc: The behavior triggered when the flight controller disarms. You have the option to trigger your motors' disarm behaviors, set all motors to coast, or set a predefined throttle setpoint

parameter.name: VTQ_DISARM_VELO
parameter.short_desc: Velocity sent when DISARM_TRIGGER is Set Predefined Velocity Setpoint
parameter.long_desc: This is the velocity that will be sent to all motors when PX4 is disarmed and DISARM_TRIGGER is Set Predefined Velocity Setpoint

parameter.name: VTQ_FC_DIR
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - If the flight controller uses 2D or 3D communication
parameter.long_desc: The FC and the ESC must agree upon the meaning of the signal coming out of the ESC. When FCs are in 3D mode they re-map negative signals. This parameter keeps the FC and ESC in agreement.

parameter.name: VTQ_MAX_VELOCITY
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - Maximum velocity when CONTROL_MODE is set to Velocity
parameter.long_desc: Only relevant in Velocity Mode. This is the velocity the controller will command at full throttle.

parameter.name: VTQ_MAX_VOLTS
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - Maximum voltage when CONTROL_MODE is set to Voltage
parameter.long_desc: Only relevant in Voltage Mode. This is the voltage the controller will command at full throttle.

parameter.name: VTQ_MOTOR_DIR
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - The direction that the module should spin
parameter.long_desc: Set the targeted motor's spinning direction (clockwise vs. counter clockwise) and flight mode (2D non-reversible vs. 3D reversible)

parameter.name: VTQ_NUM_CVS
parameter.short_desc: The number of Vertiq IFCI parameters to use
parameter.long_desc: The total number of IFCI control variables being used across all connected modules

parameter.name: VTQ_PULSE_V_LIM
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - Max pulsing voltage limit when in Voltage Limit Mode
parameter.long_desc: This sets the max pulsing voltage limit when in Voltage Limit Mode.

parameter.name: VTQ_PULSE_V_MODE
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - 0 = Supply Voltage Mode, 1 = Voltage Limit Mode
parameter.long_desc: Supply Voltage Mode means that the maximum voltage applied to pulsing is the supplied voltage. Voltage Limit Mode indicates that PULSE_VOLT_LIM is the maximum allowed voltage to apply towards pulsing.

parameter.name: VTQ_REDO_READ
parameter.short_desc: Reinitialize the target module's values into the PX4 parameters
parameter.long_desc: Setting this value to true will reinitialize PX4's IQUART connected parameters to the value stored on the currently targeted motor. This is especially useful if your flight controller powered on before your connected modules

parameter.name: VTQ_TELEM_IDS_1
parameter.short_desc: Module IDs [0, 31] that you would like to request telemetry from
parameter.long_desc: The module IDs [0, 31] that should be asked for telemetry. The data received from these IDs will be published via the esc_status topic.

parameter.name: VTQ_TELEM_IDS_2
parameter.short_desc: Module IDs [32, 62] that you would like to request telemetry from
parameter.long_desc: The module IDs [32, 62] that should be asked for telemetry. The data received from these IDs will be published via the esc_status topic.

parameter.name: VTQ_THROTTLE_CVI
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - The module's Throttle Control Value Index
parameter.long_desc: This represents the Control Value Index where the targeted module will look for throttle commands

parameter.name: VTQ_TQUE_OFF_ANG
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - Offsets pulse angle to allow for mechanical properties
parameter.long_desc: This offsets where the pulse starts around the motor to allow for propeller mechanical properties.

parameter.name: VTQ_TRGT_MOD_ID
parameter.short_desc: The Module ID of the module you would like to communicate with
parameter.long_desc: This is the value used as the target module ID of all configuration parameters (not operational parameters). The Vertiq module with the module ID matching this value will react to all get and set requests from PX4. Any Vertiq client made with dynamic object IDs should use this value to instantiate itself.

parameter.name: VTQ_VELO_CUTOFF
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - The minimum velocity required to allow pulsing
parameter.long_desc: This is the velocity at which pulsing is allowed. Any velocity between VELOCITY_CUTOFF and -VELOCITY_CUTOFF will not pulse.

parameter.name: VTQ_X_CVI
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - CVI for the X rectangular coordinate
parameter.long_desc: This represents the Control Value Index where the targeted module will look for the X rectangular coordinate.

parameter.name: VTQ_Y_CVI
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - CVI for the Y rectangular coordinate
parameter.long_desc: This represents the Control Value Index where the targeted module will look for the Y rectangular coordinate.

parameter.name: VTQ_ZERO_ANGLE
parameter.short_desc: Module Param - The encoder angle at which theta is zero
parameter.long_desc: The encoder angle at which theta is zero. Adjust this number to change the location of 0 phase when pulsing.

parameter.name: ZENOH_ENABLE
parameter.long_desc: Set true (1) to start the Zenoh driver module (a.k.a the "Zenoh-Pico Node"). See https://docs.px4.io/main/en/middleware/zenoh and https://docs.px4.io/main/en/modules/modules_driver.html#zenoh
